introSection: Lightning Rod is a hybrid roller coaster manufactured by American firm Rocky Mountain Construction and designed by Alan Schilke currently located at Dollywood in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, USA. When the ride opened on June 13, 2016, is was marketed as the world's first launched wooden roller coaster and the world's fastest wooden coaster. The track was originally wooden Topper Track, but the majority of it has since been replaced with steel I-Box Track. The prototype launch has also since been replaced with a traditional chain lift.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Lightning Rod was revealed at an event featuring Dolly Parton on August 7, 2015. Shortly before the attraction's scheduled March 19, 2016 opening, it was announced to be delayed due to launch system problems. It started technical rehearsals with guests on May 23, 2016 before officially opening on June 13.

In July 2018, Lightning Rod was temporarily closed. It reopened on October 10 after changes were made to the lead car.

In September 2020, Lightning Rod was closed for the remainder of the year. The following month, during a question-and-answer session at an enthusiasts' event, a park representative revealed that some of Lightning Rod's wooden Topper Track would be replaced with steel I-Box Track in an attempt to improve its reliability. The layout was not changed. In total, of the ride's  of track, approximately 57%, was replaced with I-Box Track during the refurbishment. The ride reopened for the first time with the new I-Box Track on March 12, 2021.

In September 2023, the park announced the removal of the ride's LSM launch due to consistent reliability issues and downtime. It reopened in 2024 with a standard chain lift hill.

sections.title: Trains
sections.content: The trains are based on hot rods.

fullText:
Lightning Rod is a hybrid roller coaster manufactured by American firm Rocky Mountain Construction and designed by Alan Schilke currently located at Dollywood in Pigeon Forge, Tennessee, USA. When the ride opened on June 13, 2016, is was marketed as the world's first launched wooden roller coaster and the world's fastest wooden coaster. The track was originally wooden Topper Track, but the majority of it has since been replaced with steel I-Box Track. The prototype launch has also since been replaced with a traditional chain lift.

Lightning Rod was revealed at an event featuring Dolly Parton on August 7, 2015. Shortly before the attraction's scheduled March 19, 2016 opening, it was announced to be delayed due to launch system problems. It started technical rehearsals with guests on May 23, 2016 before officially opening on June 13.

In July 2018, Lightning Rod was temporarily closed. It reopened on October 10 after changes were made to the lead car.

In September 2020, Lightning Rod was closed for the remainder of the year. The following month, during a question-and-answer session at an enthusiasts' event, a park representative revealed that some of Lightning Rod's wooden Topper Track would be replaced with steel I-Box Track in an attempt to improve its reliability. The layout was not changed. In total, of the ride's  of track, approximately 57%, was replaced with I-Box Track during the refurbishment. The ride reopened for the first time with the new I-Box Track on March 12, 2021.

In September 2023, the park announced the removal of the ride's LSM launch due to consistent reliability issues and downtime. It reopened in 2024 with a standard chain lift hill.



The trains are based on hot rods.
